plan_metadata.name: Feature DA Add Action And Capability Remote Debug

steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent – Agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot" option in the "New Project" dropdown to start creating a new declarative agent within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Add an Action" dropdown option in the "Create Declarative Agent" dialog of Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit within Visual Studio Code, selecting the entry to create a declarative agent with an action.
Click the "Start with a New API" option in the "Create an Action" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "None" option in the "Select an authentication type" dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "TypeScript" option in the Programming Language selection dropdown at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Default folder" selection option in the Workspace Folder dialog within the VS Code application, choosing the path "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects".

Trigger the "Command Palette" in Visual Studio Code by pressing ctrl+shift+P while in the Explorer view, which displays the project folder "VSCUSE_APP_3AVGK" and files such as package.json and tsconfig.json.

Type 'add capability' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette input field at the top center of the interface, identified by the '>' prompt and highlighted suggestion for "Microsoft 365 Agents: Add Capability".
Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Add capability" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette search bar.
Click the "Web Search" option in the "Add Capability" dialog within Visual Studio Code to select web search as the grounding information source for a declarative agent.
Click the "Search by URL" option in the "Select Search Type" dropdown at the top of the Visual Studio Code window.
Type 'https://example.com' into the "Enter an absolute URL to a site to be searched for content." input field in the Web Content dialog within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Press the Enter key to confirm the URL "https://example.com" in the URL input dialog of Visual Studio Code.
Press the Enter key to select the "manifest.json" file from the "Select Teams manifest.json File" dialog in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Add" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit confirmation dialog within Visual Studio Code to proceed with modifying files in the "appPackage" folder.

Trigger the command palette in Visual Studio Code by pressing Ctrl+Shift+P while viewing the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface, which displays account, environment, and development options in the sidebar.
Type text: 'select and start debugging' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette input box at the top center of the interface.
Click the "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette, highlighted in blue beneath the search bar.
Click the "Preview in Copilot (Chrome)" option in the Visual Studio Code command palette, located under the search input after typing "debug".
Click on the "Email or phone" input field in the Microsoft sign-in page to focus and begin entering login credentials.
Enter ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}} into the email or username input field on the Microsoft login web page under the "Sign in" section.
Click the "Next" button on the Microsoft login screen after entering the email address to proceed with the sign-in process.
Type ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}} into the 'Password' input field on the Microsoft sign-in page for the user.
Click the "Sign in" button on the Microsoft login page after entering the password, located below the password input field.
Press the Esc key to dismiss the "Save your password?" pop-up dialog in the Microsoft login page within the Chrome browser.
Click the "No" button on the Microsoft "Stay signed in?" prompt within the login.microsoftonline.com web page.
Type the text "what can you do?" into the main chat input box at the center of the Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat web interface.
Press the Enter key in the M365 Copilot chat input box after typing "what can you do?" on the main chat interface.
@assertion There are no errors on the page.
